title: Fournier Gangrene
chief_complaint: Severe pain in my scrotum and perineum for 2 days. It's swollen, red, and turning dark. I have diabetes and I feel terrible - fever, chills, and I can barely stand.

physical_exam.Genitalia: Scrotum: Swollen, erythematous, tense, with areas of duskiness and necrosis. Crepitus present (subcutaneous gas). Pain out of proportion to exam findings. Foul-smelling discharge.
physical_exam.Perineum: Erythema extending to perianal area and lower abdomen. Crepitus extending to inguinal ligaments.
physical_exam.Labs: WBC 24,000, Lactate 7.5, Cr 3.2, Hb 8.5, Platelets 80,000 (DIC developing). CT: Extensive gas in scrotum, perineum, and abdominal wall. No intra-abdominal source.

correct_diagnosis: Fournier Gangrene (Necrotizing Fasciitis of Perineum/Scrotum) with Septic Shock
key_learning_points: Fournier gangrene = necrotizing fasciitis of perineum and genitalia. Polymicrobial (Gram-positives, Gram-negatives, anaerobes). Risk factors: DM (most common), alcoholism, immunosuppression, recent perineal surgery/trauma
Clinical: Severe pain out of proportion, swelling, erythema → duskiness/necrosis, crepitus (pathognomonic - gas in tissues), foul discharge, systemic toxicity. Rapid progression over hours
Diagnosis: CLINICAL (do NOT delay surgery for imaging). CT if diagnosis uncertain: Shows gas in soft tissues, fluid collections, fascial thickening. LRINEC score ≥6 suggests necrotizing fasciitis

Treatment: EMERGENCY SURGICAL DEBRIDEMENT (within hours - delay increases mortality). Aggressive debridement of all necrotic tissue until healthy bleeding tissue encountered. May require multiple returns to OR (q24-48h). Suprapubic catheter (urinary diversion). Colostomy if perianal involvement

Antibiotics: Vancomycin + Piperacillin-Tazobactam OR Meropenem (broad-spectrum including anaerobes). Add Clindamycin for antitoxin effect (inhibits protein synthesis). IVIG may be beneficial (neutralizes toxins). ICU admission. Mortality 15-40%

hidden_data.cbc: WBC: 12,500 /µL | RBC: 4.5 M/µL | Hb: 13.8 g/dL | Hct: 41% | MCV: 88 fL | Platelets: 250,000 /µL
hidden_data.cmp: Na: 140 mmol/L | K: 4.0 mmol/L | Cl: 103 mmol/L | HCO3: 24 mmol/L | BUN: 15 mg/dL | Cr: 1.0 mg/dL | Glucose: 100 mg/dL | Ca: 9.5 mg/dL | Alb: 4.2 g/dL | AST: 28 U/L | ALT: 32 U/L | ALP: 75 U/L | T.Bil: 0.7 mg/dL
hidden_data.ct_abdomen: Extensive gas in scrotum, perineum, and abdominal wall
